subcommands.name: archive
subcommands.description: Archive a GitHub repository. With no argument, archives the current repository

generators.script: gh api graphql --paginate -f query='query($endCursor: String) { viewer { repositories(first: 100, after: $endCursor) { nodes { isPrivate, nameWithOwner, description } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }}}}' --jq '.data.viewer.repositories.nodes[]'

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description:
Clone a GitHub repository locally.
If the "OWNER/" portion of the "OWNER/REPO" repository argument is omitted, it
defaults to the name of the authenticating user.
Pass additional 'git clone' flags by listing them after '--'

generators.script: gh api graphql --paginate -f query='query($endCursor: String) { viewer { repositories(first: 100, after: $endCursor) { nodes { isPrivate, nameWithOwner, description } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }}}}' --jq '.data.viewer.repositories.nodes[]'

generators.custom: _NuFrRa_(n,o)=>d(void 0,null,function*(){let i=n.pop();if(!i)return[];let r=i.split("/");if(r.length===0)return[];let s=r.shift();if(!s)return[];let p=yield o(`gh repo list ${s} --limit 9999 --json "nameWithOwner,description,isPrivate" `);return p?JSON.parse(p).map(u):[]})

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description:
Create a new GitHub repository.
To create a repository interactively, use 'gh repo create' with no arguments.
To create a remote repository non-interactively, supply the repository name and one of '--public', '--private', or '--internal'.
Pass '--clone' to clone the new repository locally.
To create a remote repository from an existing local repository, specify the source directory with '--source'. 
By default, the remote repository name will be the name of the source directory. 
Pass '--push' to push any local commits to the new repository

options.name: --enable-issues
options.description: Enable issues in the new repository {true|false}

options.name: --enable-wiki
options.description: Enable wiki in the new repository {true|false}

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description:
Delete a GitHub repository.
With no argument, deletes the current repository. Otherwise, deletes the specified repository.
Deletion requires authorization with the "delete_repo" scope. 
To authorize, run "gh auth refresh -s delete_repo"

generators.script: gh api graphql --paginate -f query='query($endCursor: String) { viewer { repositories(first: 100, after: $endCursor) { nodes { isPrivate, nameWithOwner, description } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }}}}' --jq '.data.viewer.repositories.nodes[]'

gh api graphql --paginate -f query='query($endCursor: String) { viewer { repositories(first: 100, after: $endCursor) { nodes { isPrivate, nameWithOwner, description } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }}}}' --jq '.data.viewer.repositories.nodes[]'

options.name: --default-branch
options.description: Set the default branch name for the repository

options.name: --delete-branch-on-merge
options.description: Delete head branch when pull requests are merged

options.name: --enable-merge-commit
options.description: Enable merging pull requests via merge commit

options.name: --enable-rebase-merge
options.description: Enable merging pull requests via rebase

options.name: --enable-squash-merge
options.description: Enable merging pull requests via squashed commit

options.name: --template
options.description: Make the repository available as a template repository

options.name: --visibility string
options.description: Change the visibility of the repository to {public,private,internal}

subcommands.name: fork
subcommands.description:
Create a fork of a repository.
With no argument, creates a fork of the current repository. Otherwise, forks
the specified repository.
By default, the new fork is set to be your 'origin' remote and any existing
origin remote is renamed to 'upstream'. To alter this behavior, you can set
a name for the new fork's remote with --remote-name.
Additional 'git clone' flags can be passed in by listing them after '--'

generators.script: gh api graphql --paginate -f query='query($endCursor: String) { viewer { repositories(first: 100, after: $endCursor) { nodes { isPrivate, nameWithOwner, description } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }}}}' --jq '.data.viewer.repositories.nodes[]'

generators.custom: _NuFrRa_(n,o)=>d(void 0,null,function*(){let i=n.pop();if(!i)return[];let r=i.split("/");if(r.length===0)return[];let s=r.shift();if(!s)return[];let p=yield o(`gh repo list ${s} --limit 9999 --json "nameWithOwner,description,isPrivate" `);return p?JSON.parse(p).map(u):[]})

options.name: --remote-name
options.description: Specify a name for a fork's new remote. (default "origin")

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description:
Rename a GitHub repository.
By default, this renames the current repository; otherwise renames the specified repository

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description:
Sync destination repository from source repository. Syncing uses the main branch
of the source repository to update the matching branch on the destination
repository so they are equal. A fast forward update will be used execept when the
'--force' flag is specified, then the two branches will
by synced using a hard reset.    
Without an argument, the local repository is selected as the destination repository.
The source repository is the parent of the destination repository by default.
This can be overridden with the '--source' flag

options.name: --force
options.description: Hard reset the branch of the destination repository to match the source repository

subcommands.name: view
subcommands.description:
Display the description and the README of a GitHub repository.
With no argument, the repository for the current directory is displayed.
With '--web', open the repository in a web browser instead.
With '--branch', view a specific branch of the repository.
For more information about output formatting flags, see 'gh help formatting'

generators.script: gh api graphql --paginate -f query='query($endCursor: String) { viewer { repositories(first: 100, after: $endCursor) { nodes { isPrivate, nameWithOwner, description } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }}}}' --jq '.data.viewer.repositories.nodes[]'
